preview_text:
The model favors Japan decisively, assigning the team a 71% win probability against Tunisia's 8%. This margin reflects a substantial gap in the forecast; Japan emerges as heavy favorite in the matchup on a neutral ground.

The model's spread points toward a contest in which Japan is expected to dominate, though draws are plausible. With Japan's win probability so elevated and Tunisia's relatively low, the draw at 21% represents the most viable path to an upset scenario. The match shape suggests Japan as clear aggressor and probable winner, though not overwhelmingly so—Tunisia retains a genuine if limited chance to affect the outcome.

The model and market diverge meaningfully here. Sportsbooks assign Tunisia nearly 19% to win (10 percentage points higher than the model) and Japan only 55% (16 points lower). Polymarket prices sit between the two, favoring Japan but less heavily than the model does. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source is correct; the divergence warrants attention as a signal that underlying assessments of the teams differ materially.
